Summary: Replay "I Fought the Law" mission - Character switch after cutscene
Pre-conditions: Mission replay unlocked "I Fought the Law"
Steps:
1. Open pause menu
2. Navigate to Replay Mission
3. Select "I Fought the Law"
4. Start Mission and progress through chase sequence
5. Observe character after cutscene ends
Actual Result: Game should switch control from Franklin to Michael and spawn player in correct vehicle and location
Expected Result: Franklin remains active instead of switching to Michael, appears on police bike and later spawns in incorrect locations such as under the bridge
Post-contition: Game remains in correct character state and does not affect gameplay flow

Summary: “Father/Son” mission - Use Explosives after Jimmy is rescued
Steps:
1. Start mission “Father/Son”
2. Progress until Franklin rescues Jimmy from the boat
3. Ensure Jimmy is seated inside the vehicle
4. Trigger an explosion nearby
Actual Result: Jimmy is forcefully ejected from the vehicle, and character is launched unusually high into the air
Expected Result:
Jimmy remains seated inside the vehicle,
Explosion should not cause abnormal ejection or exaggerated physics
Post-contition: Mission progresses to next state or resets based on gameplay outcome

Summary: Free Roam - Vehicle collision should cause realistic damage and player response
Pre-conditions:
Player is in free roam mode,
Player has access to a standard vehicles
Steps:
1. Enter a vehicle.
2. Accelerate to high speed.
3. Crash the vehicle into a solid object
4. Observe vehicle damage and player reaction.
5. Repeat collisions multiple times.
Actual Result: Vehicle shows progressive visual damage like dents, broken parts, physics behave realistically, and player character reacts appropriately to impact
Expected Result: Vehicle damage system should reflect realistic collision physics with visible damage and appropriate player response after impact
Post-contition: Vehicle remains usable or becomes severely damaged depending on impact, player retains control or exits vehicle if destroyed

Summary: Free Roam -Trigger wanted level and verify police AI response and escalation
Pre-conditions:
Player is in free roam mode,
No active wanted level
Steps:
1. Perform a minor crime
2. Observe 1-star wanted level activation
3. Continue committing crimes to increase wanted level
4. Attempt to escape police line of sight
5. Hide until wanted level drops
Expected Result: Wanted system should dynamically scale police response and allow player to evade by breaking line of sight and hiding

Summary: Verify that all explosive weapons can destroy trailers in Friends Reunited mission
Pre-conditions:
Player can access mission Friends Reunited,
Player has access to multiple explosive weapons
Steps:
1. Start mission Friends Reunited
2. Progress until objective: Destroy the trailers
3. Attempt to destroy trailers using all explosive weapons
4. Observe whether trailers explode
Actual Result:
Trailers do not take damage or explode when using Railgun, Proximity Mines, Tank, Buzzard missiles, or Jet missiles, 
Trailers only explode when using Grenades, Grenade Launcher, RPG, or Sticky Bombs
Expected Result:
Trailers should be destructible by all high damage explosive weapons consistently,
All explosive weapons should trigger trailer destruction
Post-contition: Mission objective state is updated based on trailer destruction

Summary: Validate wanted level behavior during “Pizza This” odd job mission when player commits crimes
Pre-conditions:
Player is in an active online public or private session,
Player has access to and starts the “Pizza This” Odd Job mission,
No active wanted level before starting the mission
Steps:
1. Start the “Pizza This” odd job mission
2. Begin delivering pizzas
3. During the mission, commit crimes
4. Observe wanted level behavior
Actual Result:
Wanted level is not triggered during the mission despite committing crimes,
Wanted system resumes normal behavior after mission completion or failure
Expected Result:
Wanted level should trigger when player commits crimes during the mission,
Wanted system should behave consistently during and after mission
Post-contition:
Mission ends with success or failure,
Wanted system functions normally after mission

Summary: Verify vehicle paint behavior when applying Crew Color over an existing Chameleon Paint in GTA Online
Pre-conditions:
Player owns a vehicle,
Vehicle already has a Chameleon paint applied,
Player is a member of a Crew with a custom Crew Color,
Access to Los Santos Customs or Auto Shop
Steps:
1. Take the vehicle with Chameleon Paint to any Mod Shop
2. Navigate to Respray and then Primary Color
3. Select Crew Color and Confirm purchase
4. Exit mod shop
5. Switch to story mode and return back to online session for the crew color texture to appear on the vehicle  
6. Call in the vehicle and check the paint
Actual Result:
Crew Color appears selected in UI,
However, the vehicle does not retain Crew Color visually,
Chameleon Paint effect remains or overrides the applied Crew Color
Expected Result:
Crew Color should be visibly applied and persist on the vehicle,
Vehicle appearance should match the selected Crew Color consistently,
UI selection and actual vehicle paint should be in sync
Post-contition: Vehicle remains with Chameleon Paint (Crew Color not applied visually), causing mismatch between selected option and actual appearance

Summary: Verify character appearance when applying outfit components after purchasing a heist outfit
Pre-conditions:
Player owns or has access to the Heist outfits,
Player is inside a clothing store or wardrobe interaction point
Steps:
1. Open Interaction Menu or access a clothing store wardrobe
2. Navigate to Outfits
3. Purchase the outfit and Equip the purchased outfit
4. Navigate to Tops and then select any Tops
5. Observe the character model, specifically arms and upper body
Actual Result:
Character’s arms become invisible after applying “No Top” with the selected refuse collectors (the pickup) outfit combination,
Visual inconsistency observed in the character model
Expected Result:
Character model updates correctly,
Arms and upper body remain visible and properly rendered,
No visual glitches occur when combining outfit components
Post-contition:
Outfit combination (Refuse Collectors + Overcoat Blazer - No Top category) remains applied to the character,
Visual state of the character reflects the result of the customization

Summary: Verify persistence and ownership integrity of SecuroServ spawned vehicle across session transition
Pre-conditions:
Player is registered as a CEO via SecuroServ,
Player has access to CEO vehicles,
Player is currently in a populated Public Session,
No active mission running
Steps:
1. Register as CEO using SecuroServ
2. Spawn a CEO vehicle
3. Enter the vehicle and move to an active area, and ensure session sync with other players
4. While inside the vehicle, pause the game
5. Navigate to Online, Find New Session, Invite Only Session
6. Accept transition while seated in CEO vehicle
7. After loading into new session, observe player state and vehicle presence
Actual Result:
Session transition completed successfully without errors,
Player spawned correctly in Invite Only session,
Previously spawned CEO vehicle did not persist into new session,
No duplicate or ghost vehicles observed,
Player was able to spawn a new CEO vehicle normally
Expected Result:
Session transition completes without desync or crash,
Player spawns correctly in new session,
Previously spawned CEO vehicle is not improperly persisted across sessions,
No duplication of CEO vehicles occurs,
Player can spawn a fresh CEO vehicle normally
Post-contition:
Player remains registered as CEO,
No unintended vehicle persistence or duplication exists

Summary: Verify payout consistency when a business sell mission completes during session transition
Pre-conditions:
Player owns an active business with sellable stock
Player is in an active sell mission (delivery phase)
Stable connection before transition
Steps:
1. Start a business sell mission
2. Complete final delivery objective
3. Immediately trigger session change (Find New Session) during completion moment
4. Load into new session
5. Check player balance and business stock
Actual Result:
Reward credited once correctly
Stock deducted as expected
No duplication or loss observed
Expected Result:
Reward is granted exactly once
Stock is deducted correctly
No duplicate payout or rollback occur
Post-contition:
Economy remains consistent
Player progression saved correctly

Summary: Verify personal vehicle ownership and state consistency when player dies during active delivery interaction
Pre-conditions:
Player is logged into Grand Theft Auto Online
Player has an active personal vehicle (insured)
Player is engaged in a delivery-type interaction (e.g., sourcing/sell prep)
Vehicle is currently in use
Steps:
1. Begin delivery interaction using personal vehicle
2. Destroy player character (e.g., explosion) while vehicle remains in world
3. Respawn player
4. Request personal vehicle via interaction menu
Actual Result:
Vehicle ownership remained intact after respawn
Vehicle was retrievable via normal systems
No duplication, loss, or invalid state observed
Expected Result:
Vehicle ownership remains correctly assigned to player
Vehicle is either retrievable or returned to storage per design
No duplication or loss of vehicle occurs
Post-contition:
Player retains full access to personal vehicle
Vehicle system remains consistent and exploit-free

Summary: Verify consistency of business transaction, player state, and reward allocation when delivery completion overlaps with player death and session rejoin
Pre-conditions:
Player owns an active business with sellable stock
Player is in a Public Session with other players
Sell mission is initiated and in final delivery phase
Player has sufficient health/ammo to proceed
Steps:
1. Start sell mission and progress to final delivery
2. Complete partial deliveries (ensure progress is registered)
3. Initiate final delivery
4. At completion moment, trigger player death (explosion/damage)
5. During respawn/loading, initiate session change
6. Rejoin and verify balance, stock, and mission state
Actual Result:
Mission completion resolved correctly despite death and transition overlap
Reward credited once based on completed deliveries
Remaining stock updated accurately without duplication
No inconsistent mission state or UI issues observed
Expected Result:
Transaction is handled atomically (either fully completed or correctly failed)
Reward is granted only once based on final delivery state
Stock is deducted accurately (no duplication or rollback abuse)
Player death does not create inconsistent mission state
Session transition does not duplicate rewards or reset progress incorrectly
No stuck mission state or UI inconsistency
Post-contition:
Business reflects correct stock after transaction
Player balance aligns with completed delivery value
No residual mission state or exploit opportunity exists

Summary: Verify consistency of reward distribution and mission state when session host disconnects during mission completion in a co-op activity
Pre-conditions:
Minimum 2 players in same organization/MC
Co-op mission active
One player is session host
Mission is at final objective stage
Steps:
1. Start a co-op mission and progress to final objective
2. Ensure both players actively contribute (shared progress state)
3. Complete the final objective
4. At completion moment, force host disconnect (network off/quit)
5. Remaining player continues into post-mission state/session migration
6. Verify reward, mission completion, and state for remaining player
Actual Result:
Remaining player received correct reward once
Mission marked as completed successfully
No duplication or payout inconsistency observed
Session migration handled without errors
Expected Result:
Mission completion resolves correctly for remaining player
Reward is granted once per eligible player
No duplication or loss of payout occurs
Session migration does not corrupt mission state
Disconnected host does not cause rollback or duplicate rewards
Post-contition:
Player progression and earnings remain accurate
No residual or stuck mission state

Summary: Verify player health behavior when transitioning from GTA Online session to Story Mode after using a personal favorite motorcycle
Pre-conditions:
Player can switch between GTA Online and Grand Theft Auto V Story Mode,
Player owns or has a "favorite motorcycle" in GTA Online,
Player health is not already at full before starting validation
Steps:
1. Call in the favorite motorcycle using interaction menu / MC menu
2. Mount the motorcycle
3. Observe player health regeneration behavior while seated
4. Stay on the same motorcycle
5. Switch to Story Mode (Franklin / Michael / Trevor)
6. Reduce player health (eg: fall damage, NPC attack, etc.)
7. Observe health regeneration behavior after switching
Actual Result:
Regeneration persists even without being on a vehicle on Story Mode,
Effect continues across all Story Mode characters,
Effect remains active until game restart or loading a different save file
Expected Result:
Health regeneration should follow Story Mode mechanics only,
No Online specific effects should persist after switching modes
Post-contition:
Player is in Story Mode after executing the test steps,
Player health state reflects the result of the executed scenario

Summary: Verify explosion damage propagation when explosives detonate near environmental corners and obstacles
Pre-conditions:
Player has access to explosives,
Player is located in an area with solid structures (eg: buildings, walls with corners)
Steps:
1. Navigate to a location with a clear wall corner
2. Place one or more sticky bombs on one side of the corner
3. Move the player character to the next side of the corner
4. Maintain close proximity to the corner within expected blast radius
5. Detonate the explosive
6. Observe player damage and environmental impact
Actual Result:
Player does not receive any damage from the explosion when positioned around the corner,
Explosion effect does not appear to propagate beyond the obstructing surface
Expected Result:
Explosion damage should propagate realistically around corners within a certain radius,
Player should receive partial or full damage depending on distance and obstruction thickness
Post-contition:
Player remains alive and unaffected despite being within expected blast radius,
environment partially damaged
